decisions.briefing: Markets drifted slightly higher today in a quiet session — NASDAQ +0.2%, S&P 500 +0.3% with no strong directional catalyst. No trades were made today because available cash is $477.14, which falls below the $500 minimum threshold required to open a new position. The four open slots remain unfilled until cash is replenished through a profit-target exit or stop-loss trigger on one of the current six holdings.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities edged modestly higher on June 24, 2026, with the S&P 500 gaining 0.3% and the NASDAQ adding 0.2% in a low-conviction session. Homebuilders and consumer discretionary names continued to show relative strength, consistent with the strong momentum scores seen in BLDR, MHK, and several housing stocks on the screener. No major macro catalysts drove the move; the session reflected consolidation after recent gains rather than fresh directional conviction.
market_education.summary_zh: 美國股市於2026年6月24日小幅收高，標普500指數上漲0.3%，那斯達克指數上升0.2%，整體走勢溫和，缺乏明確催化劑。房屋建築商與消費類股表現相對強勢，與篩選清單中BLDR、MHK等個股的強勁動能評分一致。市場整體呈現近期漲勢後的整固態勢，投資者觀望氣氛濃厚。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Cash drag refers to the performance cost of holding uninvested cash in a rising market. Because cash earns little to nothing while stocks are climbing, a portfolio with idle cash will underperform one that is fully invested. Today's situation illustrates this: with only $477 available and four open position slots, the portfolio cannot capitalize on high-scoring momentum candidates like BLDR or RH.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「現金拖累」是指在股市上漲期間，因持有未投資現金而造成的績效損失。由於現金幾乎不產生回報，當股市走高時，持有大量閒置現金的投資組合將跑輸完全投資的組合。今日情況正是如此：帳戶僅剩477美元現金，雖有四個空置倉位，卻無法趁勢買入BLDR或RH等高動能評分個股，形成典型的現金拖累效應。
